revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Futazuka Kofun (Katsuragi)]][[ja:二塚古墳 (葛城市)]]
{{Short description|Kofun period Kofun in Japan}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = [[Zenpokoenfun]] | name = Futazuka Kofun | native_name =二塚古墳 | native_name_lang = | image = Futazuka Kofun (Katsuragi), funkyu.jpg | image_size = | alt = | caption = Futazuka Kofun | map_type = Japan Nara Prefecture#Japan | map_alt = location in Japan | relief = 1 | map_caption = Futazuka Kofun | map_size = 270px | altitude_m = | altitude_ref = | coordinates = {{Coord|34.487222|N|135.700556|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | map_dot_label = | location = {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|qid=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi, Nara|1=Katsuragi, Nara}}, {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}} | region = {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} | type = Kofun | part_of = | length = | width = | area = | volume = | diameter = | circumference = | height = | built = c.6th century | abandoned = | epochs =[[Kofun period]] | cultures = | discovered = | excavations = | archaeologists = | condition = | ownership = | management = | public_access = Yes (park) | Historic Site = | website = | architectural_styles ={{ill|keyhole-shaped kofun|qid=Q11268718|1=Zenpōkōenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳}} | architectural_details = |notes = {{box|background=white|align=center|wide=yes|border size=3px|border color=brown|text align=center|[[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]]}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|34|29|14|N|135|42|2|E|}}}}}}
{{nihongo|'''Futazuka Kofun'''|二塚古墳|}} is a [[Kofun period]] [[kofun|Kofun]], located in the Teraguchi neighborhood of the city of {{ill|Katsuragi, Nara|en|Katsuragi, Nara|qid=Q659672|lt=Katsuragi, Nara|1=Katsuragi, Nara}} in the {{ill|Kansai region|en|Kansai region|qid=Q164256|lt=Kansai region|1=Kansai region}} of {{ill|Japan|en|Japan|qid=Q17|lt=Japan|1=Japan}}. The tumulus was designated a [[Monuments of Japan|National Historic Site of Japan]] in 1972 .<ref name="Bunka1">{{cite web |url=https://bunka.nii.ac.jp/db/heritages/detail/172735|script-title=ja:文化遺産データベース|二塚古墳|language=ja |publisher=Agency for Cultural Affairs |accessdate=August 20, 2021|trans-title=Cultural Heritage Database {{!}} Futatsuzuka Kofun}}</ref>
==Overview== The Futazuka Kofun is located at an elevation of about 200 meters at the foot of {{ill|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|en|Mount Yamato Katsuragi|qid=Q11433560|lt=Mount Katsuragi|1=Mount Yamato Katsuragi}}, with a panoramic view of the {{ill|Nara Basin|en|Nara Basin|qid=Q10942269|lt=Yamato Basin|1=Nara Basin}} to the east. The tumulus is a north-facing {{nihongo|''[[zenpokoenfun|zenpō-kōen-fun]]''|前方後円墳|1=Zenpokoenfun|2=ja|3=前方後円墳|4=ko|5=전방후원분|6=zh|7=前方後圓墳|lt=Zenpokoenfun|comement=second one attempting to fix this error|lt=Zenpokoenfun|1=Zenpokoenfun|comment=doing this because I need to differentiate it in piped links too with my new interwiki tool as not the ambiguous phrase keyhole shaped kofun|1=Zenpokoenfun|1=[[Zenpokoenfun]]|2=前方後円墳|3=|4=}}, which is shaped like a keyhole, having one square end and one circular end, when viewed from above; however, it was built in a unique way, with the sides of the tumulus excavated into the high slope of the mountain.The mound is two-tiered and has a total length of 60 meters, with the posterior circular portion having a diameter of 36 meters and a height of 10 meters, the anterior rectangular portion is 41 meters wide and 10 meters high, with a projection on the west side of the narrow portion between the two portions. As both portions of the tumulus are over 10 meters high, it a squat shape and appears at first glance two circular tombs connected together, but this is because earth was piled up to create a [[:en:Burial chamber|Burial chamber]] in both sections of the tumulus. There is a deep moat on the east side of the tumulus, and a shallow moat on the west side. No ''{{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|qid=Q849376|lt=Haniwa|1=Haniwa}}'' have been found, but ''[[fukiishi]]'' roofing stones were laid in key areas.<ref name="Shiseki">{{cite book |last1=Isomura |first1=Yukio |last2=Sakai |first2=Hideya |title=(国指定史跡事典) National Historic Site Encyclopedia |date=2012 |publisher=学生社 |isbn=978-4311750403}}{{in lang|ja}}</ref>
The existence of a stone burial chamber in the circular posterior mound has been known since ancient times, but an [[archaeological excavation]] in 1958 revealed that there were also burial chambers in the anterior portion and the western extension. The posterior burial chamber is 16.7 meters long including the passageway. The burial chamber proper is 6.73 meters long, 2.98 meters wide, and 4.1 meters high, and opens to the south. A drainage ditch has been built in the passageway. The passageway is made of piled-up natural stones. It is believed that a {{ill|Tuff|en|Tuff|qid=Q484924|lt=Tuff|1=Tuff}} sarcophagus was placed in the burial chamber, but it has been destroyed and details are unknown. Although the burial chamber has been open since ancient times, and excavations have unearthed gilt bronze flower-shaped seating brackets, horse equipment, iron weapons, farming tools, and beads.
The anterior burial chamber is 9 meters long, including the passageway. The burial chamber proper is 3.9 meters long, 1.7 meters wide, and 1.9 meters high. Only the bottom part of the tuff composite sarcophagus remains. Horse equipment, farming tools, and gold and silver hollow beads have been excavated.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The stone chamber in the western projection is a type also found in parts of the [[:en:Korean peninsula|Korean peninsula]]. The chamber has a total length of 7.82 meters, while the burial chamber proper measuring 4.48 meters long, 1.35 meters wide, and 1.26 meters high. It is unusual in that the burial chamber is built one step lower (about 0.9 meters) than the antechamber. Excavations have unearthed {{ill|Amber|en|Amber|qid=Q25381|lt=Amber|1=Amber}} beads, straight iron swords, iron knives, horse equipment, iron arrowheads, iron axes, iron sickles, iron spades, and a large amount of {{ill|Sue pottery|en|Sue pottery|qid=Q7634351|lt=Sue pottery|1=Sue pottery}}. As this chamber had not been looted, a total of 118 {{ill|Grave goods|en|Grave goods|qid=Q1399692|lt=Grave goods|1=Grave goods}} were found packed tightly together, which led to the theory that this was a stone chamber for burying grave goods. However, as there was a space of about two meters between the artifacts, it is believed that a wooden coffin had once been placed there.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
The excavated items are stored at the {{ill|Nara National Museum|en|Nara National Museum|qid=Q147312|lt=Nara National Museum|1=Nara National Museum}}, and suggests that the tumulus was built in the late Kofun period, from the early to mid-6th century. It is about a 30-minute walk from {{ill|Kintetsu Shinjō Station|en|Kintetsu Shinjō Station|qid=Q6414374|lt=Kintetsu Shinjō Station|1=Kintetsu Shinjō Station}} on the {{ill|Kintetsu Railway|en|Kintetsu Railway|qid=Q1531085|lt=Kintetsu Railway|1=Kintetsu Railway}} {{ill|Gose Line|en|Gose Line|qid=Q4792932|lt=Gose Line|1=Gose Line}}.<ref name="Shiseki"/>
==See also== *{{ill|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|en|List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|qid=Q17050860|lt=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)|1=List of Historic Sites of Japan (Nara)}}
==References== {{reflist}}
==External links== {{template-inline|Futazuka Kofun (Katsuragi)}} *[https://www.city.katsuragi.nara.jp/soshiki/rekishihakubutsukan/1/1/1835.html Katsuragi City home page] {{in lang|ja}}
{{Kofun navbar}}
